name: Azuaga Circular via Granja de Torreherm
name_original: Granja de Torrehermosa y Berlanga — circular desde Azuaga
description: This moderate 48.9-mile (78.7 km) road cycling route from Azuaga explores charming villages and serene landscapes with minimal traffic.
region_intro: Road cycling around Azuaga features routes that traverse the undulating terrain of the region, characterized by rolling hills and open landscapes. The area's geography includes varied elevation gains, with some routes featuring significant climbs. Distances for road cycling routes are generally long, connecting rural towns and offering extended rides through the countryside. The landscape primarily consists of agricultural areas interspersed with small settlements. Best road cycling routes around Azuaga The most popular road cycling route is Azuaga Circular via Granja de Torreherm, a 48.9 miles (78.7 km) trail that takes 3 hours 24 minutes to complete. This moderate route offers a circular journey through the local countryside. Another top favourite among local road cyclists is Puerto de Llerena loop from Valverde de Llerena, a difficult 45.2 miles (72.8 km) path. This route includes substantial elevation gain, indicative of the hilly terrain in the area. Local road cyclists also love the Fuente Obejuna Town Center – Fuente Obejuna loop from Azuaga, a 46.4 miles (74.7 km) trail leading through rural landscapes and past the town center, often completed in about 3 hours 36 minutes. Road cycling around Azuaga is defined by rolling hills, open countryside, and rural roads. The network offers options for different ability levels, from moderate to difficult. The routes in Azuaga are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.4 stars from over 7 reviews. More than 200 road cyclists have used komoot to explore Azuaga's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: What is the general difficulty level of road cycling routes around Azuaga?
A: The road cycling routes in Azuaga offer a range of difficulties, from moderate to difficult. You'll find options suitable for experienced cyclists looking for a challenge, as well as several moderate routes. Out of 14 available routes, 8 are classified as moderate and 5 as difficult.

Q: Are there any road cycling routes in Azuaga suitable for beginners or those looking for an easier ride?
A: While most routes are moderate to difficult, there is one route classified as easy. For those seeking a less strenuous experience, it's advisable to check the elevation profiles and distances carefully, as even moderate routes can feature significant climbs in this region.

Q: What kind of distances can I expect on road cycling routes near Azuaga?
A: Road cycling routes around Azuaga generally feature longer distances, connecting rural towns and offering extended rides through the countryside. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e992861288/azuaga-circular-via-granja-de-torreherm" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Azuaga Circular via Granja de Torreherm&lt;/a&gt; is 48.9 miles (78.7 km), and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/20122085" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Granja de Torrehermosa – Fuente Obejuna loop from Azuaga&lt;/a&gt; extends to 61.5 miles (98.9 km).

Q: Do the road cycling routes in Azuaga feature significant elevation gain?
A: Yes, the region around Azuaga is characterized by rolling hills, and many routes include substantial elevation gain. For instance, the difficult &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/20122445" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Puerto de Llerena loop from Valverde de Llerena&lt;/a&gt; has over 1,000 meters of ascent, indicative of the hilly terrain you'll encounter.

Q: What is the best time of year for road biking in Azuaga?
A: The best time for road biking in Azuaga is typically during the spring (March to May) and autumn (September to November) when temperatures are milder. Summers can be very hot, making early morning or late afternoon rides preferable if cycling during this season. Winters are generally mild but can be damp.

Q: Are there any loop road cycling routes available around Azuaga?
A: Yes, many of the road cycling routes around Azuaga are designed as loops, allowing you to start and end in the same location. A popular example is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e992861288/azuaga-circular-via-granja-de-torreherm" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Azuaga Circular via Granja de Torreherm&lt;/a&gt;, which offers a circular journey through the local countryside.

Q: What kind of scenery and landmarks can I expect to see along the road cycling routes in Azuaga?
A: The routes primarily traverse agricultural areas interspersed with small settlements, offering views of open countryside and rural landscapes. You might pass through towns like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5332798" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Granja de Torrehermosa&lt;/a&gt; or near historical churches such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5884075" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Church of Our Lady of Consolation&lt;/a&gt;, providing glimpses into the region's heritage.

Q: What do other road cyclists enjoy the most about road cycling in Azuaga?
A: The road cycling experience in Azuaga is highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.4 stars from over 7 reviews. Cyclists often praise the varied terrain, the challenge of the rolling hills, and the opportunity to explore the open countryside and rural roads.

Q: Are there any less crowded road bike trails in Azuaga?
A: The region's road cycling routes generally offer a peaceful experience through rural landscapes. While specific crowd levels can vary, routes that connect smaller villages or venture further into the countryside, such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/20122453" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Fuente Obejuna – Church of Ojuelos Altos loop from Azuaga&lt;/a&gt;, are likely to be less frequented than those closer to Azuaga itself.

Q: Are there any family-friendly road bike trails in Azuaga?
A: Given the prevalence of moderate to difficult routes and significant elevation gains, most road cycling trails around Azuaga are better suited for experienced cyclists. There is one route classified as easy, which might be more appropriate for families or those seeking a gentler ride. Always check the route details, including distance and elevation, before setting out with family.

Q: Where can I find parking for road bike trails in Azuaga?
A: Azuaga, being a rural town, typically offers various parking options within the town center or near the starting points of routes. It's advisable to look for public parking areas in Azuaga or the smaller towns you plan to start from, such as Valverde de Llerena or Fuente Obejuna, which usually have accessible street parking.

Q: Are there cafes or places to stop for refreshments along the road cycling routes?
A: Many routes connect or pass through small towns and villages, such as Granja de Torrehermosa or Fuente Obejuna. These settlements typically have local cafes, bars, or shops where you can stop for refreshments, especially in their town centers. Planning your route to include these stops can enhance your cycling experience.
tips: The town is located southeast of the Province of Badajoz, bordering the Autonomous Community of Andalusia through the province of Córdoba. Its church is one of the most well-known buildings and at the same time beautiful in the nucleus.
A surprising church once you enter the center of Berlanga, not only because it contrasts with the white houses of the town but also because of its tower, which can be seen from afar.

faqs.question: How long does it take to cycle the Granja de Torrehermosa and Berlanga — circular from Azuaga route?
faqs.answer: This racebike route is approximately 78.7 kilometers long and typically takes around 3 hours and 25 minutes to complete. This duration can vary based on your cycling pace and any stops you make along the way.

faqs.question: What is the terrain like on this cycling route?
faqs.answer: The route is primarily on firm, paved surfaces suitable for road cycling. While generally well-maintained, be prepared for varied terrain as some sections in the broader region can involve significant climbs. The overall elevation gain for this specific route is around 448 meters.

faqs.question: Is this cycling route suitable for beginners?
faqs.answer: The route is classified as 'moderate' in difficulty. While it's a road cycling route, its length (78.7 km) and elevation gain (around 448 meters) mean it requires good physical fitness. Beginners with some experience in longer rides might find it challenging but rewarding.

faqs.question: Where does the route start, and is there parking available?
faqs.answer: The route is a circular one starting from Azuaga. While specific parking details aren't provided, Azuaga, being a town, should offer public parking options. It's advisable to check local parking availability upon arrival.

faqs.question: What can I see along the way?
faqs.answer: The route passes through charming towns and offers diverse landscapes. You'll cycle through Granja de Torrehermosa, a village known for its 'extremeño character' and notable architecture, including the impressive Church of Our Lady of Grace. The general area features agricultural lands like olive groves and cereal fields.

faqs.question: When is the best time of year to cycle this route?
faqs.answer: The region, being in southern Spain, can experience hot summers. Spring and autumn generally offer more pleasant temperatures for cycling. These seasons provide comfortable weather to enjoy the scenery and local culture without extreme heat.

faqs.question: Do I need any permits or pay entrance fees for this route?
faqs.answer: No, there are no specific permits or entrance fees required to cycle this public road cycling route. You can enjoy the trail freely.

faqs.question: Is this cycling route dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: As a road cycling route, it's generally not suitable for dogs to accompany cyclists directly on the road due to traffic and safety concerns. If you plan to bring a dog, it would be best to consider alternative activities or ensure they are safely transported.

faqs.question: Are there places to stop for refreshments or accommodation along the route?
faqs.answer: Yes, the route passes through towns like Azuaga, Granja de Torrehermosa, and Berlanga. These villages offer opportunities for traditional stops, where you can find cafes, pubs, and potentially accommodation to experience the local culture and refuel.

faqs.question: What should I consider regarding weather when planning this ride?
faqs.answer: The region can get very hot, especially in summer. It's crucial to check the weather forecast before your ride. In warmer months, plan to start early to avoid the midday heat, carry plenty of water, and wear appropriate light and breathable cycling attire. In cooler seasons, layers are recommended.

faqs.question: Are there any specific challenges or unique aspects to be aware of on this route?
faqs.answer: One notable aspect of cycling in this region is the opportunity to enjoy serene and peaceful roads with minimal traffic, allowing for an immersive experience of the breathtaking scenery. While the route is moderate, be prepared for some climbs. The chance to experience local culture through traditional village stops is a highlight.
